name: resolver-b3-edge-routing-struct
description: ADR 0037 Phase V — edgeRouting struct resolves deterministically across Minimal→Standard→Minimal. Wave 3 expanded EdgeRoutingOptions from a single mode field to a full RouteOptions struct so the resolver could control bow, segments, lod knobs declaratively. Test: apply Minimal, snapshot edgeRouting, apply Standard, apply Minimal again, snapshot. The two Minimal snapshots must be byte-identical (deterministic resolution). We do NOT assert Standard differs from Minimal — factory defaults overlap with what Minimal declares (both yield `mode: 'straight'` etc.), which is fine and not an invariant violation. The original 'must differ' assertion was a buggy invariant the v1 suite caught.

commands: (async () => { window.__canaryApplyProfile('minimal'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400)); const r1 = window.__canaryGetEdgeRouting().value; const baselineSerialized = JSON.stringify(r1); window.__canaryApplyProfile('standard'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400)); window.__canaryApplyProfile('minimal'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400)); const r3 = window.__canaryGetEdgeRouting().value; if (JSON.stringify(r3) !== baselineSerialized) throw new Error('edgeRouting struct not deterministic across Minimal→Standard→Minimal. First: ' + baselineSerialized + ' Third: ' + JSON.stringify(r3)); return 'B3 pass: edgeRouting struct deterministic across Minimal→Standard→Minimal'; })()
